task:
Ticket 0014 — wire the remaining dead 'Pick folder' button so the native directory picker works on the workspace detail editor too.

CONTEXT (already done in a prior run — do NOT rebuild): the New-Workspace CREATION modal is fully wired end-to-end and tested. `packages/ui/electron/workspace-picker.ts` implements `pickWorkspaceDirectory` (native `dialog.showOpenDialog({properties:['openDirectory']})` + `validateWorkspacePrimaryRoot` mirroring the daemon's primary-root rules), exposed via `ipc-contract.ts`/`main.ts`/`preload.ts` as `window.oz.workspaceDirectoryPick()` and `window.oz.workspacePrimaryRootValidate(path)`. In `packages/ui/app/App.tsx`, `handlePickWorkspaceRoot()` (around line 429) already calls `oz.workspaceDirectoryPick()` and returns `{ path: string|null, error?: string }`. `NewWorkspaceModal` (packages/ui/app/sections/modals.tsx) already consumes it via the `onPickRoot` prop with a working `pickRoot()` handler and inline error display. Leave ALL of that exactly as-is.

THE GAP TO FIX: In `packages/ui/app/sections/Workspaces.tsx`, the `RootRow` component's folder button (line ~26: `<button className="oz-iconbtn" ... title="Pick folder"><Icon name="folder-notch-open" .../></button>`) has NO onClick — clicking it does nothing. This is the same dead-picker defect the ticket describes, on the workspace detail editor's per-root path field. Wire it to the SAME existing picker the modal uses. No new IPC, no daemon change, no new picker logic — reuse what exists.

WHAT TO CHANGE (thread the existing handler down App -> WorkspacesScreen -> RootRow):
1. `App.tsx`: pass the existing `handlePickWorkspaceRoot` to `<WorkspacesScreen .../>` (line ~686) as a new prop, e.g. `onPickRoot={handlePickWorkspaceRoot}`. Do not write a new handler — reuse `handlePickWorkspaceRoot`.
2. `Workspaces.tsx` `WorkspacesScreen`: accept the new optional prop `onPickRoot?: () => Promise<{ readonly path: string | null; readonly error?: string }>` and forward it to each `RootRow`.
3. `Workspaces.tsx` `RootRow`: accept `onPickRoot`. On the folder button, add `type="button"` and an `onClick` that (a) does nothing if `onPickRoot` is absent or a pick is already in flight; (b) calls `onPickRoot()`; (c) on a returned `path`, updates the row via `onChange({ ...root, path })`; (d) on a returned `error`, shows it inline in the row. Mirror the modal's UX: a local `picking` state that disables the button while the dialog is open (`disabled={!onPickRoot || picking}`), and clear any prior error before re-picking. Add a minimal inline error element in the row (reuse the modal's styling idiom: `className="oz-field-help"` with `color: 'var(--cb-error)'`) so a rejected pick (path missing / not a directory / inside the install root) is visible — the error text comes straight from the picker result, do not re-implement validation.

MUST NOT BREAK:
- The New-Workspace creation modal flow and `handlePickWorkspaceRoot`/`handleValidateWorkspaceRoot` stay byte-for-byte unless threading the prop requires a touch.
- Keep the existing RootRow grid layout, the resolvedPath display, the role dropdown, the 'exactly one primary' logic, and all current styling. The new error line must not shift the layout when absent.
- `onPickRoot` is OPTIONAL — when it is undefined (non-Electron/test render) the button must be inert and render without crashing, exactly like the modal handles a missing `onPickRoot`.
- No changes under `packages/ui/electron/**`, `packages/daemon/**`, or `ipc-contract.ts`.

WRITE-SCOPE: `packages/ui/app/sections/Workspaces.tsx`, `packages/ui/app/App.tsx`, and (optional, encouraged) a test under `packages/ui/tests/`.

TESTS / EXIT CRITERION (report the exact commands you ran and their full output): (1) the UI package typecheck passes; (2) the existing UI test suite stays green (run the vitest suite for packages/ui). If a test runner script name is unclear, inspect `packages/ui/package.json` scripts and the repo root scripts. Strongly preferred: add a focused test (alongside `packages/ui/tests/workspaces-sync.test.ts` or `modal.test.tsx`) that renders the workspace editor / RootRow, clicks the folder button with a stub `onPickRoot` returning a path, and asserts the row's path updates; and a second case where `onPickRoot` returns an `error` and asserts the inline error renders. If rendering RootRow in isolation is awkward, a direct unit test of the wiring is acceptable — state in your report what you covered and why.

Report: the diff summary, the commands run with output, and one line on the non-primary-root edge case (the shared picker applies primary-root validation to every row; note whether that is acceptable for now — do NOT expand scope to per-role validation in this atom).

Ticket 0014 — make the fix 'verified on evidence' RUNNABLE: author a one-command proof harness `scripts/proof-workspace-picker.mjs` that proves the directory-picker -> create -> scaffold chain at every automatable seam.

WHY: Ticket 0014's code is complete and committed (5d70fcc). Its acceptance criterion 4 demands the fix be 'verified on evidence'. This repo discharges that with proof harnesses, NOT manual checklists — see the existing `scripts/proof-*.mjs` (e.g. `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs`). Follow that house pattern EXACTLY: a small Node script (node builtins only — `child_process.execFile`, `node:path`, `node:url`, `node:util`) that RUNS THE REAL EXISTING TESTS proving each invariant and prints a PASS/FAIL table, exiting nonzero if any row is red. It must NOT reimplement the picker, validation, daemon, or scaffold, and must NOT modify any product or test code — it only runs tests and tabulates.

STUDY FIRST: read `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` end-to-end and mirror its structure, style, header-comment format, table rendering, and exit-code behavior. Match how it shells out to a package's vitest and maps each suite/file to the behavior it protects.

ROWS THE TABLE MUST COVER — each row must map to a REAL, currently-passing, named test file; run that file (e.g. `pnpm --dir packages/<pkg> exec vitest run <relative-test-path>`), parse pass/fail, and render a row:
1. Native dialog contract + primary-root validation — `packages/ui/tests/workspace-picker.test.ts` (asserts `showOpenDialog` is called with `properties:['openDirectory']`; rejects missing path / non-directory / path inside the install root; accepts a valid outside path and returns an absolute path — mirrors the daemon's `validateWorkspaceRootRules`).
2. Detail-editor folder-button wiring — `packages/ui/tests/workspaces-screen.test.tsx` (folder button fills the row's path from the picker, shows the picker's validation error inline without changing the path, and is inert when no picker is available).
3. Creation-modal folder-button wiring — LOCATE the existing test that exercises `NewWorkspaceModal`'s `onPickRoot`/`onValidateRoot` path (check `packages/ui/tests/modal.test.tsx`, `app.test.tsx`, `live-app.test.tsx`, `workspaces-sync.test.ts`). Map this row to whichever real test actually asserts that wiring. If NO existing test isolates the modal picker wiring, DO NOT invent a passing row and DO NOT add a test in this atom — instead render the row as a clearly-labeled GAP (e.g. status 'UNCOVERED') and note it in the harness header + your report, so the founder sees the one seam tests don't yet cover.
4. Downstream create -> scaffold -> commit -> visible-in-registry — the `createWorkspace` / scaffold cases in `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts` (scaffolds the full `cocoder/` skeleton into a fresh primary root, rejects a primary nested inside the install root, and the new workspace is retrievable). Map to the real test file; if the relevant cases are best targeted by name, use vitest's `-t` filter, otherwise run the whole file.

HEADER COMMENT (house style): one line invocation `node scripts/proof-workspace-picker.mjs`; what an all-green table means (ticket 0014's picker fills the path field, the picked path is validated against the API rules with inline errors, and submit creates+scaffolds+commits `cocoder/` — proven at every automatable seam); and ONE honest sentence that the only seam a headless harness cannot render is the live OS dialog actually drawing on screen (a ~30s founder click in the Electron app), which the `showOpenDialog` contract test (row 1) stands in for.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERION (report exact commands + full output):
- `node scripts/proof-workspace-picker.mjs` runs from the repo root, prints a readable PASS/FAIL table, exits 0 when all mapped tests pass (or exits nonzero / shows red if any row's test fails or row 3 is an explicit GAP — your call which, but be consistent and documented in the header).
- Every PASS row corresponds to a real test that actually exists and actually passes — verify by also running each underlying test command yourself and pasting output.
- The script adds NO dependencies and modifies NO file other than creating `scripts/proof-workspace-picker.mjs`.

MUST NOT BREAK: do not touch product code, existing tests, or other `scripts/*`. Pure additive new file.

WRITE-SCOPE: `scripts/proof-workspace-picker.mjs` only.

Report: the new file's full content rationale (1-2 lines), the exact commands you ran with output (the harness run + each underlying test command), and explicitly state whether row 3 (creation-modal wiring) maps to a real test or is an honest GAP, naming the test file if found.
